description_long: This is a large, single-story log cabin constructed primarily from thick, rounded timber logs with a dark, gabled roof. The structure features a prominent covered porch at the front, supported by sturdy wooden posts, and a stone foundation visible along the base. Multiple windows with dark frames are symmetrically arranged along the visible facades, providing ample natural light to the interior. The roof is steeply pitched with overhanging eaves and a chimney protruding from one side, suggesting a fireplace inside. A wooden deck extends from one side of the cabin, offering additional outdoor space. The overall design is rustic and robust, evoking traditional log cabin architecture suited for both rural and suburban settings.
description: A large, rustic log cabin with a dark gabled roof, covered front porch, stone foundation, and multiple symmetrically arranged windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, main entrance, and part of the stone foundation beneath the log walls.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the front porch, main entrance, and the side extension with additional roof coverage.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the long log wall, stone foundation, and a wooden deck extending from the left side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back gable, several windows, and a small covered side porch.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with a steep pitch, overhanging eaves, and a single chimney; constructed from dark roofing material
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple medium-sized rectangular windows symmetrically distributed along the front, rear, and side facades; grouped in pairs or threes with dark frames
attribute_door_placement: Single main door centered on the front facade beneath the porch; possible secondary door on the side extension visible from the rear view
